summary:
Several household items, including a cane, chair, mirror, and portrait, are said to be haunted by the spirits of people who died under tragic circumstances.
These items, often referred to as "haunted" or "cursed," are believed to be responsible for strange occurrences and unexplained events.
The items in question include a chair that is said to have belonged to Napoleon, a mirror that contains the trapped souls of a slave master's family, and a portrait of a we

A mother and daughter survived a car accident in Melbourne, Australia, where three of their children died after their car plunged into a lake.
The mother, Akon Guode, and her 5-year-old daughter, Aluel Manyang, were rescued from the water and taken to hospital.
The children's father, Joseph Manyang, believes his wife may have been distracted and lost control of the car, and is cooperating with police in their investigation.

Cardiothoracic surgeon and TV host Mehmet Oz has successfully defended himself against criticism from 10 doctors who accused him of promoting "quack treatments" for personal gain.
Oz's team responded with a segment on his show, highlighting the critics' own questionable motives and credentials.
The doctors' letter was seen as a failed attempt to discredit Oz, who has a strong media presence and a large following.
Columbia University has defended Oz, saying he has the right to express

Google has updated its Android handsets to recognize handwriting in 82 languages and 20 scripts, allowing users to input text without typing.
The update also enables users to draw emojis and send them directly to contacts.
Google has also launched a new tool that allows users to locate their lost phone by searching for "find my phone" on the search engine's homepage.
The tool can pinpoint the phone's location and remotely call it, but cannot lock or erase the phone's data.

A team of geophysicists has discovered a massive secondary magma chamber beneath Yellowstone National Park, which is 11,035 cubic miles in size and could fill the Grand Canyon 11 times over.
The chamber, which sits 12-28 miles beneath the supervolcano, contains 98% hot rock and 2% molten rock, too deep to cause an eruption.
The discovery could provide clues about the structure of other supervolcanoes and help researchers understand the

At 80 years old, Jan Bearman got her first tattoo in memory of her daughter Shell, who died of diabetes complications in 2011.
Jan has since gotten a new tattoo every birthday to commemorate her daughter, with the latest being a coloured shell below her daughter's name.
The tattoos are a memorial for Jan and a way for her to carry her daughter with her, and she has become a tattoo enthusiast despite initially not being a fan.
Jan's decision to get tattoos

A 12-year-old King Charles Spaniel named Alfie suffered third-degree burns from a heated pad used during an MRI scan at an Animal Health Trust clinic in Newmarket, Suffolk.
The pad had been warmed in a microwave and placed directly on Alfie's back, causing severe burns.
The clinic has since changed its practice to use an incubator to heat the pads and has promised to monitor them more closely.
Alfie is now on medication to heal his wounds, but

Kim Kardashian is saving her luxurious designer wardrobe for her 22-month-old daughter North West, creating an "archive" of her most iconic outfits. The reality star plans to hand down her entire wardrobe to her daughter when she gets older, including her Givenchy wedding dress. Kim believes in encouraging her daughter's individuality and unique style, saying "be yourself and take risks".

Bulgaria has been named Europe's cheapest destination for summer holidays, with Black Sea resorts offering the best value for meals and other activities.
The country's strong pound and lower inflation have made it an attractive option for travelers, with prices 13.6% lower than last summer.
Other affordable options include the Algarve in Portugal and Turkey, while Sorrento in southern Italy is the most expensive, with prices 2.5 times higher than Bulgaria's.

A 61-year-old US mailman, Doug Hughes, landed his gyro-copter on the US Capitol lawn, breaching a no-fly zone, to protest against government corruption and campaign finance reform.
The stunt, which was undetected until authorities saw the craft approach, was met with a strong police response, with Hughes being arrested and bomb disposal robots deployed.
Hughes claimed he had informed the Secret Service and Capitol police of his plan, but they were not aware of the

A sheep-herding dog named Stains received a lion-like makeover from its groomers, with its coat shaved to resemble a lion's mane.
The dog's owner was initially surprised by the transformation, but laughed hysterically as Stains walked around the house in its new look.
The shaved coat, combined with the dog's natural coloring, gave Stains a majestic appearance, prompting the owner's laughter and the attention of other dogs in the house.
The video captures Stains

Motherwell will fight the SPFL over free entry to the Premiership play-offs for season-ticket holders if they finish 11th.
The club's general manager, Alan Burrows, says they will not charge season-ticket holders for the play-off final if they make it to the tie.
Rangers and Hibernian are also set to back Motherwell in their revolt against the SPFL's 50% gate levy on play-off games.
The SPFL had previously withdrawn a bid

Niall Horan caddied for Rory McIlroy at the Masters and will return the favor by singing on stage with him at Gillette Stadium in Massachusetts in September.
Horan will perform with McIlroy after the Northern Irish golfer competes at the Deutsche Bank Championship in Boston.
The pair made a deal that if Horan caddied for McIlroy, McIlroy would sing with One Direction on stage.
McIlroy has never performed in public

A chef visits Grenada, finding inspiration in the island's fresh produce and local recipes.
Grenada offers a variety of exotic fruits, vegetables, and spices, making it a culinary paradise.
The island's beaches, markets, and restaurants provide opportunities to experience the local cuisine and culture.
Visitors can also explore the island's rum distilleries and plantations, such as the River Antoine Rum Distillery and Belmont Organic Farm.

The Eshima Ohashi bridge in Japan is a steep, one-mile-long bridge with a 6.1% gradient, making it a challenging hill start for drivers.
It is the third-largest rigid frame bridge in Japan and the third-largest in the world.
The bridge's steep slope gives it a rollercoaster-like appearance and was recently featured in a commercial to test a minivan's durability.

A Washington teen was suspended from school for 5 days after wearing a fake explosive vest to propose to his date at prom. The teen, Ibrahim Ahmad, claims the prank was meant to be funny and part of a trend, but was deemed inexcusable by the school. Ahmad, who is of Middle Eastern descent, believes the punishment was unfair and felt racist. The prom has been ruined for Ahmad and his date, Rilea Wolfe, who had accepted his proposal.

A University of South Carolina student has been suspended after a Snapchat photo showed her writing "n*****s" as the reason for the campus's poor Internet connection on a whiteboard.
The student, a former member of the Kappa Delta Chapter sorority, was identified by fellow students and condemned for the slur.
The university's president has stated that the incident has challenged the community to reflect on its values and that racist and uncivil rhetoric have no place at the university.
The

Ashley Barnes was sent off for a "criminal challenge" on Nemanja Matic, sparking controversy.
Everton beat Burnley 1-0 with Kevin Mirallas scoring the only goal.
Referee Mike Jones denied Burnley a penalty for a challenge on Ross Barkley, sparking criticism from manager Sean Dyche.

A 49-year-old grandmother, Patricia Ebel, was arrested after crashing her luxury car into another vehicle in Naples, Florida, and failing a sobriety test. Ebel, who was driving her 10-year-old grandson home, had a blood alcohol content of.15 or greater and refused a breathalyzer test. She faces DUI charges, including driving with a minor in the car, and her car was totaled in the crash. Neither Ebel nor her grandson was injured

An 11-year-old boy was beaten to death by his school head teacher for stealing a pencil and eraser.
The head teacher, Lalit Verma, has been arrested and charged with culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
The boy's severe beating is not an isolated incident, with reports of similar cases of corporal punishment and child deaths in Indian schools.
The cause of death is currently being investigated, but police suspect internal bleeding.

Queen Margrethe of Denmark celebrated her 75th birthday with a two-day celebration, including a gala banquet and fireworks display at Fredensborg Castle.
The Danish royal family, including Crown Prince Frederik, Crown Princess Mary, and Prince Joachim, attended the event, as did royals from other European countries.
The celebration began with a family wake-up call, followed by a balcony appearance, and ended with a procession down Copenhagen shopping street Strøget.
The event

Everybody Loves Raymond actor Sawyer Sweeten, 19, took his own life in Texas while visiting family.
No signs of depression or warning signs were reported prior to his death, according to his manager.
The show's cast, including Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton, paid tribute to Sweeten, calling him a "wonderful and sweet kid".
Sweeten's death has sparked a call for people to check in with loved ones and prioritize mental health support.

The average British worker attends 6,239 meetings in their career, with 60% finding most meetings "pretty pointless". Many workers zone out or fall asleep during meetings, with 70% admitting to constantly disengaging. The survey also found that 45% of employees believe their company wastes money on travel for meetings, and 37% admit to fiddling expenses. The study suggests that technology, such as conference calls, can be a more efficient way to communicate

Formula One driver Lewis Hamilton has opened up about the meaning behind his tattoos in an interview with Men's Health magazine.
Hamilton, a two-time world champion, has several tattoos that reflect his faith, love of music, and family values.
The Mercedes driver emphasizes the physical demands of F1, stating that his heart rate can reach up to 190 beats per minute during a qualifying lap.
Hamilton credits his intense training regimen for his success, having lost six kilos last year to meet

Amateur astronomer captures photo of sun spot resembling a giant yolk.
Sun spot is a common phenomenon caused by magnetic fields and surface temperature reduction.
The sun spot can be hundreds of times larger than the Earth and has a temperature of 2,700-4,200°C.
Gordon Ewen, 57, captured the photo using his telescope in his garden in Hertfordshire, England.

Photographer James Oatway captured a brutal attack in Johannesburg's Alexandra Township, where a Mozambican man was beaten and stabbed to death in broad daylight.
The attack, which took less than two minutes, was part of a wave of xenophobic violence targeting foreign immigrants in South Africa.
Oatway's photos of the attack, which were published in the Sunday Times, have sparked outrage and led to the arrest of the four suspects.
The incident has highlighted the deep-seated

Demolition of Archway Sheet Metal Works begins, final hurdle cleared for Tottenham's new stadium development.
The £400m project, which will replace White Hart Lane, is expected to be completed in 2018.
The stadium's design includes a one-tier stand and a bowl-shaped bowl, similar to Arsenal's Emirates Stadium.
Tottenham will face Aston Villa at the Lane on Saturday, with the team hoping to be ready to move into their new stadium in 2018.

Experts identify six warning signs of a potentially toxic partner.
They include lack of self-awareness, emotional unavailability, dishonesty, immaturity, self-loathing, and a negative outlook.
These traits can lead to an unhealthy and potentially damaging relationship.
Being aware of these signs can help women protect themselves from heartbreak.

A bear in Alaska's Katmai National Park was unable to catch a salmon that leaped into the air, allowing the fish to escape.
The bear, waiting at the best position in the falls, was too slow to react and let the salmon continue its journey.
The salmon, which migrate to Alaska's rivers to spawn, are vulnerable to bears and eagles, but can be difficult to catch underwater.
The bear, a veteran of many failed attempts, was unlucky on this occasion

ABC is developing a proof of concept for a revival of "The Muppets" with "The Big Bang Theory" co-creator Bill Prady co-writing the script.
The project, if successful, could lead to a series order and would feature the original Muppet characters gathering for a meeting at ABC Studios.
The show would include new roles and celebrity cameos, but would not move forward without Miss Piggy's involvement.
The revival marks Prady's second attempt to revive

Sinn Fein president Gerry Adams sparked outrage with comments on US TV about the abduction and murder of Jean McConville, describing it as 'what happens in wars'.
McConville's family and relatives of IRA victims have condemned Adams' remarks, with her daughter Helen McKendry calling him a 'liar' and saying he has 'blood on his hands'.
Adams denied involvement in McConville's murder and claimed he was not a member of the IRA

Crystal Palace manager Alan Pardew denies interest in signing Tim Krul from Newcastle.
Pardew says he's happy with current goalkeeper Julian Speroni and has no contact with Newcastle or Krul.
There's been no discussion about a transfer between Pardew, Newcastle, or Krul's agent.

The Queen is hiring a sous chef to work at Buckingham Palace and other Royal residences.
The successful applicant will have to travel and work with unique ingredients from the Royal gardens and estates.
The job pays around £28,000-a-year and includes accommodation, meals, and a comprehensive benefits package.
The Queen's former personal chef revealed her simple tastes in food, including Special K, jam sandwiches, and chocolate cake.

The final resting place of Lizzie Borden and her family was vandalized at Oak Grove Cemetery in Fall River, Massachusetts, with black and green paint.
The vandalism was discovered on Monday, the day after a Lifetime miniseries about Borden premiered on Sunday night.
This is not the first time the plot has been vandalized, with previous incidents including graffiti, pennies, flowers, and other items.
The cemetery director is seeking to catch the perpetrators and is urging people to

Young people in Iran are defying the country's hardline Islamic image to create a more Westernized society, with many openly embracing Western culture and lifestyles.
The Iranian youth is among the most politically active groups in the country, with over 63% of the population under 30, and represents a significant threat to the current theocratic rule.
The will of young people in Iran has also influenced the country's changing relationship with the US, with many young Iranians expressing support for the

A couple in Oregon has been awarded $240,000 for over a decade of noise complaints from their neighbors' barking Tibetan mastiffs.
The dogs' owners, John Updegraff and Karen Szewc, were ordered to debark the canines within 60 days to reduce the noise.
The jury ruled the dogs were unsuited to protecting livestock, despite the owners' claims they were needed for this purpose.

A driver in Gloucestershire followed his sat nav down a country lane, losing the roof of his lorry suspended in the air between a hedge and a tree. The incident occurred on an unregistered road, and police are considering pursuing the driver for a driving offence. A picture of the incident was tweeted to warn other drivers to be cautious on country roads. The driver's sat nav led him astray, highlighting the importance of checking vehicle height.

Rev. Robert H. Schuller, a Southern California televangelist and author, has died at 88 after a long battle with illness.
Schuller's ministry, which included the Crystal Cathedral, collapsed in recent years due to a leadership transition and financial struggles.
He was known for his positive and inclusive message, which emphasized the importance of love and redemption, and was a popular figure among both conservative and liberal Christians.
Schuller's legacy is complex, with some

A light aircraft crashed near Loch Etive in western Scotland, and the bodies of two people were found amid the wreckage.
The plane was reported missing around 30 miles east of Oban, and a rescue operation was launched, involving lifeboats, helicopters, and police.
The aircraft was believed to have been travelling from central Scotland to Tiree, the most westerly island of the inner Hebrides, when it lost radar contact.
The police are investigating the incident and urging

Indiana has seen a significant increase in HIV cases, with 120 confirmed cases and 10 preliminary positive cases in Scott County, amid a short-term needle exchange program approved by Gov. Mike Pence. The program, which provides clean syringes to intravenous drug users, has seen a surge in participation, with 5,322 syringes distributed since its approval. Pence opposes needle exchanges as an anti-drug policy, but health officials say they are a proven harm-reduction

Reading goalkeeper Adam Federici had already booked his wedding for the weekend of the FA Cup final, which would have clashed with the match against Arsenal.
Federici's error in the semi-final led to a 2-1 loss for Reading, and he was left in tears on the pitch.
Despite the setback, Federici has apologized to fans and expressed his willingness to learn from the mistake, and Reading boss Steve Clarke expects him to bounce back.
Federici's wedding is

The NCAA is now saying it has no legal responsibility to ensure the academic integrity of courses offered to student-athletes, despite its previous claims that education is paramount.
The organization's stance is being challenged by a lawsuit filed by former University of North Carolina athletes, who claim they were not getting an education due to the academic fraud scandal.
The NCAA's new position is seen as a contradiction to its previous rhetoric, which has emphasized the importance of education for student-athletes.
Crit
Sanjay Chaddah, a property developer, was found guilty of falsely accusing his neighbor's son-in-law of a racist assault and was given a six-month suspended sentence for common assault and perjury.

The owners of two dogs that mauled a jogger to death in Michigan have pleaded no contest to owning a dangerous dog causing death and will serve 15 years in jail. The dogs, cane corsos, had attacked at least two neighbors before killing 46-year-old Craig Sytsma in July. The owners, Sebastiano Quagliata and Valbona Lucaj, could be deported after serving their sentences and have been in custody for months. The Sytsma family

Cesc Fabregas scored a late goal to give Chelsea a 1-0 win over QPR at Loftus Road, moving Jose Mourinho's team seven points clear at the top of the table.
The goal, Fabregas' first on target in the game, came in the 88th minute and was enough to secure the win.
Chelsea manager Jose Mourinho praised his team's mentality and determination, saying they "always play to the end".
The win marked a

Chelsea will face Stoke City at Stamford Bridge on Saturday, with Diego Costa's hamstring injury status uncertain.
Stoke's Jonathan Walters is expected to return from a calf injury, while Victor Moses will not play due to his parent club being Chelsea.
Chelsea have won 10 and lost just one of the 13 Premier League meetings with Stoke City, and have kept nine shutouts in 13 matches against them.

Toby Alderweireld will return to Atletico Madrid at the end of his loan spell at Southampton.
The Belgian defender has impressed this season and has hinted at a desire to stay in the Premier League.
Atletico Madrid's sporting director Jose Luis Perez Caminero says the club is counting on Alderweireld's return.
Alderweireld's contract expires in June 2017.

Tottenham will subsidise Emmanuel Adebayor's £100,000-per-week wages to allow him to leave the club this summer.
The striker is out of favour under manager Mauricio Pochettino and will enter the final 12 months of his deal.
Adebayor will be made available for loan or a permanent move, with a loan switch potentially offering a lucrative free transfer in 2016.
The 31-year-old has made just nine league starts this

Australian celebrity chef Pete Evans is under fire for promoting a reworked baby milk formula in his cookbook that contains high levels of vitamin A, which could be hazardous to babies. The formula, called "Happy Tummy Brew," was originally criticized for containing too much vitamin A, but Evans' co-authors claim it has been reworked to be safer. Despite criticism from health experts, Evans has been promoting the benefits of the paleo diet, which has been linked to improved health outcomes

A study found that exposure to Fisher-Price magnets in the 1970s and 1980s may have caused a generation to associate certain letters with specific colors.
At least 6% of participants in the study learned their letter-color pairings from the magnets, with some born in the 1970s and 1980s having up to 15% of their matches from the toy.
The study suggests that synesthesia, a neurological condition where two senses are joined

A typed surrender order issued by German president Karl Doenitz on May 8, 1945, is being auctioned for £21,000.
The order, which instructed German officers to abandon fighting or face the Allies' wrath, was seized from Luftwaffe chief Robert von Greim when he was arrested that day.
Von Greim, a WWI fighter ace, killed himself rather than be tortured by the Russians after being arrested and taken to Salzburg.
The

Juan Mata's fortunes have changed under Louis van Gaal, who has brought out the best in the midfielder after a disappointing spell at Chelsea. Mata has scored in his last five games and is performing at the peak of his powers, averaging more goals and assists than ever before in the Premier League. The 29-year-old has formed close bonds with teammates Ander Herrera and David de Gea, and has found a new sense of freedom playing in a three-man defense. Mata's

BMW Mini has unveiled concept smart specs, called Augmented Vision goggles, that project virtual displays onto the road for drivers to see directions and other features.
The goggles, which resemble vintage driving goggles, can also be worn out of the car and show information such as speed limits, local landmarks, and parking spaces.
The technology aims to boost safety by providing drivers with relevant information in their direct field of vision without obscuring other road users.
The goggles are connected to a smartphone and can

Former paramedic Crsity Collins used ice while driving and treating patients to raise awareness about the dangers of the drug.
Collins, who was addicted to ice, said she was never offered support despite checking herself into the emergency room multiple times while experiencing psychosis.
She credits intensive help from the Department of Veterans Affairs with her nine-month sobriety and now wants to raise awareness about the "epidemic of ice users" in Tasmania.

Women throughout history have used various methods to beautify themselves, from ancient Egypt's donkey-milk baths to 19th-century Austria's homemade face creams.
Cleopatra bathed in donkey-milk, while Nefertiti used lead-based kohl and crushed beetles as blusher.
Other ancient cultures, such as Greece and India, used ingredients like honey, olive oil, and cow dung to achieve beauty goals, including pale complexions and blonde hair.

An Israeli team of developers has created a 'thermal protection' case called Amited that warns and cools down phones when they get too hot.
The case uses micro-fans to blow heat away from the device or heats it up using resistance coils if it gets too cold.
Amited syncs with a smartphone over Bluetooth and tracks temperature changes, allowing users to set custom operation modes and set alarm thresholds.
The case is designed to keep smartphones working at their best, regardless of temperature

Three men in their 50s from the York area have been arrested on suspicion of murdering missing chef Claudia Lawrence.
The arrests are linked to ongoing investigations into her disappearance, which began in 2013.
Detectives are treating her disappearance as a suspected murder and are seeking to uncover evidence that could lead to her body being found.
The new arrests bring the total number of people arrested in connection with the case to four.

Kazakhstan's President Nursultan Nazarbayev has won re-election with 97% of the vote, sparking international concern over the lack of a genuine choice of candidates.
Nazarbayev apologized for not allowing a more modest victory, saying it would have looked undemocratic.
The election was held more than a year early to avoid economic instability and keep investors confident.
Kazakhstan's economy has been hurt by declining energy prices and economic difficulties in neighboring Russia.

Model Stephen James, 24, is becoming one of the most in-demand models despite having a tough exterior and numerous tattoos.
He has lucrative contracts with Calvin Klein and Diesel, and has starred in numerous men's magazine shoots and runway shows.
James says he doesn't let his tattoos define him, and that they're about expression and staying grounded, not impressing others.
He's recently single and is enjoying the attention, but also values his private life and has portrait tattoos of Oscar

Maryland parents Danielle and Alexander Meitiv are suing authorities after their children were picked up by police and held for nearly six hours without food.
The couple, who practice "free-range" parenting, claim authorities crossed a line by keeping them in custody for so long.
They were cited multiple times for allowing their children to roam free in their neighborhood, but say the latest incident was excessive and unjustified.
The Meitivs are seeking damages for violating their fundamental rights as parents

Aritz Aduriz scored a last-gasp equaliser for Athletic Bilbao to deny Valencia victory.
The draw took Valencia above Sevilla into fourth place in the pursuit of Champions League qualification.
Getafe moved 10 points clear of the bottom three with a 1-0 win over Elche, while Espanyol beat Villarreal 3-0 with two goals from Felipe Caicedo.

Italian navy retakes control of seized fishing boat off Sicily coast.
Gunmen had seized the boat, Airone, carrying 7 fishermen, 3 Sicilians, and 4 Tunisians, 5 days earlier.
Rifle shots were accidentally fired, injuring one fisherman, and the Libyan soldier on board was taken into custody.
The incident highlights the ongoing issue of migrants being smuggled across the Mediterranean.

Jason McDonald, 34, from Colorado, walked a slackline over a pool of over 50 hungry alligators at the Colorado Gator Farm.
The stunt was part of a new way for McDonald to interact with the reptiles, which he has been handling for over 10 years.
McDonald's parents have reservations about his daredevil antics, but he plans to continue his hobby, including walking over the farm's largest alligators.

Twitter suspended over 10,000 accounts linked to Islamic State militants and their supporters in a single day.
The purge was prompted by reports of violent threats and propaganda on the platform.

Jade Wimsey, 24, lost 6 stone in 18 months by quitting energy drinks and adopting a healthy diet.
She ballooned to a size 20 after consuming up to 7 energy drinks a day, despite initially thinking they were harmless.
Quitting energy drinks and adopting a healthy lifestyle gave Jade a newfound confidence and energy.
She now weighs 11 stone 6.5lbs and is helping others through her blog.

Graeme Finlay, 53, is accused of attacking an elderly couple on a luxury cruise ship, claiming he acted in self-defense after being attacked by the husband, Ron Phillips, wielding a crutch.
Finlay said he was ignored when he joined their table for dinner and felt awkward, leading to a confrontation that escalated into violence.
The couple, Ronald and June Phillips, suffered serious injuries, including facial injuries and two fractured vertebrae, and Finlay was "

Two brothers, Jeff and Joey Stallings, fell seriously ill after smoking synthetic marijuana in Mississippi.
The brothers, both in their twenties, were hospitalized with hallucinations, vomiting, and violent shaking, and were placed in medically-induced comas.
They have since recovered, but their mother, Karen, warns that the drug is highly addictive and can cause permanent damage, including kidney damage and brain damage.
Karen Stallings is now appealing to others not to smoke synthetic marijuana, also known as

Liverpool's Jordan Ibe is set to return to action after a six-week absence due to a knee injury.
Ibe has been training at a high level and will be included in the squad to face Newcastle on Monday.
Manager Brendan Rodgers is optimistic about Ibe's return, saying he's "looked absolutely sensational" in training.
The team will face Newcastle without Mario Balotelli, who is still recovering from illness and injury.

Colin Heath, 43, was jailed for three years and two months for manslaughter after admitting to violently shaking a baby girl, Kealeigh-Anne Woolley, in 2000, leaving her permanently disabled and blind. The child spent most of her life in a wheelchair and died in 2011 at the age of 11. Heath's actions were described as "catastrophic" and "isolated" by his defense, but he was found guilty of manslaughter

Former Fortune 500 executive Robert Dellinger, 54, was sentenced to 20 years in prison for causing a fatal car crash that killed a Vermont couple and their unborn baby in 2013. Dellinger pleaded guilty to negligent homicide and assault, and was given credit for 15 months already served, which could reduce his sentence to less than 8 years. The crash occurred when Dellinger's truck crossed the median and smashed into an SUV carrying the couple, who were from

The bilby, Australia's native Easter mascot, is under threat due to low numbers, with only small populations remaining in Queensland, the Northern Territory, and Western Australia.
The bilby's population has declined dramatically, with the species now extinct in New South Wales, Victoria, and South Australia.
Predators like feral cats and foxes, as well as habitat destruction and competition with introduced species, are major threats to the bilby's survival.
A captive breeding program is being

A massive underground city in Turkey's Cappadocia region has been discovered, consisting of 3.5 miles of tunnels, secret churches, tombs, and safe havens. 
The subterranean city, dating back around 5,000 years, is believed to be the largest in Cappadocia, with early estimates suggesting it could be around a third bigger than the famous Derinkuyu underground city.

Photographer Anthony Barbour, 33, from Liverpool, creates 360-degree photographs of British locations by taking 50 pictures and stitching them together.
He spends hours rotating in one spot to capture the surroundings, from floor to sky, and uses a Canon 1100d DSLR to shoot the images.
The resulting pictures showcase surreal and mind-bending views of locations such as Sefton Park, Mudeford Beach, and Durdle Door.
Barbour uses the

A fireball that crashed near the Russian-Finnish border last year shares an orbit with a large asteroid found in October.
Researchers used camera footage to recreate the fireball's trajectory and found it was similar to asteroid 2014 UR116, which is due to pass by the moon in 2017.
The asteroid is estimated to be 1,100lb and poses no immediate threat, but its size and potential for damage make it a concern.
The study suggests that streams

A bomb technician created beeping Easter eggs for blind children to hunt, allowing them to participate in the traditional Easter activity.
The device, developed by David Hyche, uses a transmitter to produce a high-pitched beeping sound inside plastic eggs.
The Alabama Institute for Deaf and Blind in Birmingham has adopted the idea and hosted an Easter egg hunt for blind children.
The event allows children with disabilities to participate in a fun and inclusive activity.

An 8-year-old abuse survivor, Marie Surprenant, wrote a heartfelt letter to her social workers and detectives thanking them for helping her find a happy home after being severely beaten as an infant.
Marie, who is now in a wheelchair, credits her social workers and detectives for solving her case and giving her a safe and loving environment.
She expressed gratitude for the care and support she received, saying her life is "so much better" and that she is now a

Freddie Gray, a 25-year-old black man, died one week after being taken into police custody in Baltimore, with the cause of death still unclear.
Gray's family and attorney claim police involvement in a cover-up, alleging that Gray was chased and arrested without probable cause.
Police are declining to release details about the case, citing ongoing investigations, and have placed the officers involved on administrative duties.
Protesters are demanding answers, following a string of recent deaths involving white officers

Jameela Jamil had high mercury levels in her blood after having her amalgam fillings removed, which she believes caused her health problems for nearly seven years.
She spent £3,000 on having her fillings replaced with white fillings, but her symptoms persisted and she continued to experience fatigue, headaches, and digestive issues.
Dentists disagree that mercury from fillings can cause health problems, citing a lack of evidence and the fact that mercury vapour is released during

Cities around the world have unique nicknames that reveal their history, industry, and environment. Research by Just The Flight found that nicknames like Las Vegas's "Sin City" and Singapore's "Lion City" originated from specific events or characteristics of the city. Some nicknames, like Philadelphia's "City of Brotherly Love," were chosen by the city's founder, while others, like Amsterdam's "Venice of the North," are based on the city's physical features

Australian cricket legend Richie Benaud has died at 84 after a battle with skin cancer.
Benaud was a renowned television broadcaster and commentator, known for his dry wit and knowledge of the game.
He was also a skilled player, winning 63 Test caps and scoring 2,201 runs, and played a key role in changing the way cricketers were paid.
Benaud's legacy will be remembered for his style, which was characterized by his laconic

Michelle Knight, one of the three women held captive by Ariel Castro for almost 10 years, has spoken out about her healing process and refuses to be considered a victim, saying she wants to "overcome and flourish" from her experiences.
Knight revealed she has a new relationship and is working on a second book about her life since being rescued in 2013.
She has also started writing music, including a song called "Survivor", and is learning to play the piano.

More than 200 Yazidi prisoners were released from Islamic State captivity in northern Iraq.
The prisoners, including 40 children, were led to believe they were being executed but were instead freed.
They were taken to peshmerga positions and received medical care and treatment after being held captive since last summer.
The reason for their release was not given, but the freed Yazidis are in poor health and bearing signs of abuse and neglect.

A speed camera in Birmingham, England, has been angled to point at a house after being left unattended for two years.
The camera, which was part of a 2013 effort to decommission old technology, was not working due to its outdated system.

Researchers suggest that a warming climate 1.4 million years ago enabled early humans to migrate from Africa to Western Europe.

Bayern Munich's squad returned to Germany after a surprise 3-1 loss to Porto in the Champions League quarter-final first leg.
The team's injuries and fatigue were cited as factors in the defeat, with Bayern having played with only 15 outfield players due to several key players being injured.
Coach Pep Guardiola expressed disappointment but vowed to try and overturn the deficit in the return leg.

The US Postal Service issued a Forever stamp honoring Maya Angelou, featuring a quote attributed to her, but it was later revealed that the quote actually came from Joan Walsh Anglund.
The quote, "A bird doesn't sing because it has an answer, it sings because it has a song," was widely attributed to Angelou, but she herself used it in a 1967 book and it was also used in a children's book before her autobiography was published.

Mississippi Senator Thad Cochran's personal assistant, Fred Pagan, has been charged with trafficking methamphetamine and date rape drugs from China.
Pagan, 49, was paid $160,000 by Cochran's office last year and has worked for the senator since he was 16.
He faces up to 40 years in prison for possession and intent to distribute methamphetamine and up to 20 years for importing a controlled substance.

Aston Villa and Queens Park Rangers are set to face off in a crucial Premier League match that could determine the fate of both teams' seasons. The match is a "sliding doors" moment, with Villa needing a win to stay above the relegation zone and QPR hoping to escape the bottom three. The two managers, Tim Sherwood and Chris Ramsey, have a personal connection, having worked together at Tottenham Hotspur, and will be on opposite sides of the pitch

Floyd Mayweather vs Manny Pacquiao's live stream could be watched for free on social media apps like Periscope and Meerkat.
Broadcasters are concerned that millions could be lost if viewers stream the fight without paying.
Periscope and Meerkat's terms of service claim they do not support copyright infringement, but rely on user reports to address the issue.

Ten top doctors have urged Columbia University to remove Mehmet Oz from his faculty position due to his promotion of 'quack treatments' and'miracle' weight-loss supplements.
The doctors claim Oz's advice endangers patients and is driven by personal financial gain.
Columbia University has defended Oz, stating it values academic freedom and upholds faculty members' freedom of expression.
Oz has denied being a 'charlatan' and claims to provide balanced information without conflict of interest.

A Canadian nurse, Joanna Flynn, has been charged with manslaughter and criminal negligence after allegedly turning off a patient's life support without permission. The patient, Deanna Leblanc, 39, died at the hospital in 2014 after undergoing a minor knee operation. The hospital's CEO described the charge as "highly distressing" and said it would create anxiety for the community. Flynn has been released on bail and will appear in court again on May 28.

A man fell asleep on the baggage reclaim conveyor at Domodedovo Airport in Russia.
Other passengers seemed more concerned with collecting their luggage than the sleeping man.
The incident is not the first of its kind, with similar cases reported at other airports.
The man was taken through the X-ray scanner after the conveyor started working.

A 20-year-old woman was shot in the neck while driving on a Colorado interstate, with a bullet passing through her neck without damaging vital organs.
The shooting may be linked to other unexplained reports of shattered windows on highways in northern Colorado.
Deputies are investigating and urging drivers to report suspicious behavior.
The woman is expected to recover from her injuries.

Campaign group Let Toys Be Toys launched #NotAnAprilFools to expose sexist toys in stores.
Images show girls offered pink beautician's outfits and boys doctor's uniforms, with polarized products for infants.
The group is petitioning retailers to end labeling products by gender, citing the harm of perpetuating stereotypes.
Experts say the practice reinforces objectification of women and limits children's potential.

Paris Saint-Germain will face Nice without key players Thiago Silva, Zlatan Ibrahimovic, Marco Verratti, and Thiago Motta due to injuries and illness.
The team will still start with David Luiz, who is recovering from a thigh problem, and coach Laurent Blanc is urging his players to be "championship mode".

Former NFL cornerback Will Allen and his business partner Susan Daub are facing civil fraud charges over a multi-million dollar Ponzi scheme.
The scheme allegedly promised high returns to investors by funding loans to cash-strapped pro athletes.
The SEC alleges that Allen and Daub paid investors with other investors' money, rather than actual profits, and used some funds for personal expenses.
The agency is seeking an injunction and unspecified penalties against the pair and their firms.

Robert Durst, 71, pleaded not guilty to two state gun charges in Louisiana, delaying his extradition to California to face murder charges. Durst waived extradition to California, but the weapons arrest has kept him in New Orleans. He faces charges in the 2000 death of a longtime friend and is being held without bail in a state prison. Durst's lawyers argue that the arrest was timed to coincide with the final episode of a documentary about his life.

Ronen Ziv, an Israeli man, is waiting anxiously in Israel for news about his surrogate mother, Nafisa Shaje, who is due to give birth in Kathmandu, Nepal, after a devastating earthquake. Ziv's unborn baby is in a breech position and requires a cesarean section, but hospitals in Nepal are overwhelmed with injured quake victims. Nadine Crooks, 33, is expecting triplets after being told she was infertile due to polycystic ovary syndrome after her fourth child.
The mother of four was shocked to discover she was pregnant again after stopping taking contraceptives.
She is now expecting two boys and a girl, who will be siblings to her four existing children.

An Icelandic duo has created a snack bar called the Jungle Bar, made with cricket flour, dates, sesame seeds, and chocolate.
The bar is claimed to be a good source of iron and protein due to the cricket flour.
The duo hopes to encourage people in the West to eat more insects as a sustainable alternative to traditional protein sources.
The Jungle Bar is available for purchase on Kickstarter for $25, with shipping expected to begin in August 2015.

Model Jesinta Campbell and wellness blogger Belle Gibson are among hundreds of thousands of followers who feel insulted by Gibson's deception about her cancer diagnoses. Gibson has admitted to faking her multiple cancer diagnoses and has said she doesn't want forgiveness. Campbell expressed her disappointment and shock at Gibson's actions, saying she felt Gibson had let everyone down. Gibson's business empire and relationship with her fans are now in tatters.

Radio host John Burns has been accused of calling Richmond's Bachar Houli a 'terrorist' at a club function.
Burns, who hosts 3AW's Ross and John, denies making the comment but apologizes for any offense caused.
A complaint was made against Burns, and he claims he doesn't recall making the comment, but acknowledges it was mortifying to him.
Richmond Football Club has released a statement accepting Burns' apology and considering the matter closed.

A clumsy tortoise's attempt to mate with a female ended in embarrassment as it lost its balance and fell onto its back in front of its mate. The tortoise, seen in a series of hilarious photos, took 15 minutes to crawl out of the mud after failing to impress its partner. Former rap mogul Suge Knight pleaded not guilty to murder and other charges in a fatal hit-and-run incident in Compton, California, where he allegedly ran over two men, including a former rap music label owner, Terry Carter, who later died. A pit crew member, Todd Phillips, was injured when struck by a car during the IndyCar Grand Prix of Louisiana.
Phillips, a tire changer for Dayle Coyne Racing, was clipped by Francesco Dracone's car on Lap 25.
He was taken to the infield care center for treatment and released after receiving stitches for a cut on his leg.
Dracone, who spun out, did not finish the race and ended up 23rd.

A banyan tree in southern China has a unique, web-like structure of roots that has captured the attention of locals. The tree's roots grew up against a wall before being uprooted and reattached to create the unusual trunk. The web of roots is surprisingly flimsy, causing the tree to sway in the wind. This is not the first time such a tree has been spotted in Guangxi province, with another similar tree found in a public park last year.

Liverpool midfielder Philippe Coutinho believes winning the FA Cup would save their season.
The Reds trail fourth-placed Manchester City by seven points with seven games remaining in the Premier League.
Coutinho hopes to qualify for the Champions League for a second consecutive season, but acknowledges the top four finish is unlikely.
Winning the FA Cup would be a consolation prize, but Coutinho says it's still their primary objective.

David Cameron's election campaign walkabout in Alnwick was disrupted by heckling, with one woman telling him to 'f*** off back to Eton'. Despite this, the Prime Minister was met with positive reactions from voters, who praised his efforts to engage with ordinary people.

Eugenie Bouchard took a break from tennis to relax with friends, including Olympic medalist Kerri Walsh Jennings and Sports Illustrated model Hannah Ward.
The Canadian tennis star was seen in good spirits after a string of early losses, including a second-round exit at the Miami Open.
Bouchard has struggled since reaching the quarter-finals of the Australian Open in January, but seems to be taking her mind off the game.

A six-year-old boy was taken away from his parents due to his poor dental health, which led to the removal of 13 teeth. The boy's parents, who are Latvian, accepted responsibility for his poor dental hygiene caused by a high sugar diet. The boy will remain in foster care and decisions about his long-term future will be made by local authority officials. The case highlights the growing concern of tooth decay in children, with nearly 500 being admitted to hospital each

A red Mazda crashed into the front of a house in Crossgates, Leeds, becoming stuck in the wall.
No one was seriously injured in the accident, but the occupants suffered minor injuries.
The driver of the Mazda has been reported and police are investigating the incident.
The car's front end was completely destroyed in the crash, but it remained stuck in the wall.

Paul Gregory, 51, of Bedford, was fined £795 for dishonestly obtaining a blue badge, claiming he could only walk 200 metres when in fact he was a keen walker and had completed a 191-mile hike. 
Council officials discovered Gregory's deception after reading a newspaper article about his hiking exploits in Cumbria and Wales. 
Gregory pleaded guilty to two counts of dishonestly obtaining a blue badge and was ordered to carry out 150 hours of unpaid work

Bubba Watson, the reigning Masters champion, was voted the most unpopular player on the PGA Tour by his peers in an ESPN.com survey.
Watson received 22.6% of the votes, with Patrick Reed coming in second and Rory Sabbatini third.
Watson took the results in stride, saying he needs to "improve as a man" and prove his peers wrong.
